title: malus domestica var. roseus
intro: Malus domestica var. roseus, also known as the rose crabapple, is a variety of apple tree that is renowned for its fragrant, pink or white flowers and edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Malus domestica var. roseus lie in its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The health benefits of consuming Malus domestica var. roseus fruits include supporting immune function, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting overall well-being. Malus domestica var. roseus can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
